adds.rationale: Pure-play US LNG exporter Cheniere brought its Corpus Christi Stage 3 expansion online in December 2024, adding capacity right as Europe's reliance on US LNG deepened with the Ukraine-Russia gas transit agreement set to expire Jan 1, 2025 - filling the geopolitical-energy bucket the profile names but the watchlist currently does not cover.

news_evidence.summary: Two new US LNG export terminals (Cheniere Corpus Christi Stage 3 and Venture Global Plaquemines) began operation in December 2024, adding 30 mtpa to total US export capacity.
Front-month natural gas futures hit a 52-week high of $4.20/MMBtu by late December 2024, driven by cold weather demand and European reliance on US LNG as Ukraine-Russia gas transit deal nears expiry.

removes.rationale: ARKX is a diluted rockets/spacecraft vehicle (top holdings include Trimble, Iridium, Kratos - more aero/comms than pure launch); we trim it now to make cap room for a higher-conviction geopolitical-energy add and can re-express rockets exposure via a pure-play (e.g., RKLB) in a later quarter when warranted.

news_evidence.summary: Rocket Lab completed 16 Electron launches in 2024, becoming the pure-play rockets name the broader thesis is really about, while ARKX's diversified-aerospace basket has lagged.

rationale_overall: One swap at the cap: add LNG (Cheniere) to plug the geopolitical-energy / LNG-exports bucket the profile names but the watchlist had zero coverage of, and remove ARKX since it is a diluted rockets vehicle whose constituents do not really map to the user's rockets/spacecraft thesis. Net effect: the watchlist trades a soft thematic ETF for a concrete pure-play on the Russia-Ukraine driven LNG demand shift, while still holding AI (MSFT, NVDA), robotics (ISRG, BOTZ), aging-demographics (LLY), quantum (QTUM), and nuclear (NLR).
